one_line: HubSpot provides a comprehensive suite of APIs for interacting with its CRM, marketing, sales, service, and CMS hubs, supporting various use cases like data integration, custom apps, and workflow automation. Authentication methods include OAuth 2.0 and API keys.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (recommended for public apps and integrations), API key (legacy, deprecated for new private apps), and Private Apps (scoped API keys for specific developer accounts).
self_serve: Self-serve with Developer Account. Developers can create a free developer account and start building apps. Access to certain features or higher API limits may require a paid HubSpot account.
api_surface: Extensive. APIs cover CRM (Contacts, Companies, Deals, Tickets, Products, Quotes), Marketing (Email, Forms, Landing Pages, Blogs, Social, Workflows), Sales (Engagements, Calling, Meetings, Playbooks), Service (Feedback Surveys, Knowledge Base), CMS (Pages, Themes, Files), Automation (Workflows), Reporting, Events, and more.
mcp: Official HubSpot Developer Community forums and HubSpot Slack Community.


one_line: Pipedrive offers a comprehensive REST API for integrating with their CRM platform, allowing programmatic access to deals, organizations, persons, activities, products, and more using OAuth 2.0 or API tokens.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (recommended for third-party apps), API Token (for private integrations/scripts)
self_serve: Self-serve with Pipedrive account required; developers can generate API tokens and manage OAuth apps via their account settings.
api_surface: Deals, Organizations, Persons, Activities, Products, Users, Pipelines, Stages, Notes, Files, Webhooks, Custom Fields, Campaigns, Leads, Email messages, Mail threads, Project, Templates, Call logs. Extensive CRUD operations are available.
mcp: No clear official Pipedrive MCP, but a developer community forum is available.

one_line: Salesforce provides a comprehensive suite of APIs including REST, SOAP, Bulk, Metadata, Streaming, and Tooling APIs to interact with CRM data, customize platform features, and integrate with external systems.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (web server, user-agent, username-password, JWT bearer, device, refresh token flows), Connected Apps for integration with external applications.
self_serve: Self-serve trial (developer orgs available for free sign-up and API access), then Gated/paid enterprise (requires a Salesforce org with API access enabled, which usually comes with paid editions).
api_surface: CRM data manipulation (CRUD on standard/custom objects via REST/SOAP/Bulk API), metadata deployment and retrieval (Metadata API), real-time eventing (Streaming API), platform configuration and customization (Tooling API), analytics (Analytics API). Supports webhooks via Apex Triggers and Platform Events.
mcp: No clear MCP (Salesforce Trailblazer Community and Stack Overflow are primary community channels, but not an MCP server like Discord/Slack for API support).

one_line: Stripe provides APIs for internet businesses to accept payments, send payouts, and manage their business online, offering a comprehensive suite of payment processing and financial services.
auth: API Key (Secret Key and Publishable Key). Secret keys are used for server-side operations and should be kept confidential. Publishable keys are for client-side operations (e.g., collecting card details). Stripe also supports Webhooks for event notifications.
self_serve: Self-serve trial. Developers can sign up for a Stripe account and immediately obtain API keys for testing and development. Live mode requires activation with business information.
api_surface: Payments (one-time, recurring), Subscriptions, Connect (platform payments), Billing, Invoicing, Checkout, Radar (fraud protection), Sigma (data analytics), Issuing (card creation), Treasury (financial accounts), Tax, Identity verification, Refunds, Disputes, Payouts, Financial Reporting, Webhooks.
mcp: Stripe Developers community forum, Stack Overflow, Twitter.

```json

  "category": "Finance and Fintech",
  "one_line": "Stripe provides APIs for internet businesses to accept payments, send payouts, and manage their business online, offering a comprehensive suite of payment processing and financial services.",
  "auth": "API Key (Secret Key and Publishable Key). Secret keys are used for server-side operations and should be kept confidential. Publishable keys are for client-side operations (e.g., collecting card details). Stripe also supports Webhooks for event notifications.",
  "self_serve": "Self-serve trial. Developers can sign up for a Stripe account and immediately obtain API keys for testing and development. Live mode requires activation with business information.",
  "api_surface": "Payments (one-time, recurring), Subscriptions, Connect (platform payments), Billing, Invoicing, Checkout, Radar (fraud protection), Sigma (data analytics), Issuing (card creation), Treasury (financial accounts), Tax, Identity verification, Refunds, Disputes, Payouts, Financial Reporting, Webhooks.",
  "mcp": "Stripe Developers community forum, Stack Overflow, Twitter.",
  "verdict": "Buildable",
  "blocker": "None",
  "evidence": "https://stripe.com/docs/api\nhttps://stripe.com/docs/keys\nhttps://stripe.com/docs/webhooks\nhttps://stripe.com/docs/developer-resources\nhttps://stripe.com/docs/api-reference"
}
```
